---
title: "Day 4: AI in the Physical World"
author: "Simon Guest"
format:
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    incremental: true
    center-title-slide: true
    embed-resources: true
    theme: [default, ../../theme/dark.scss]
    logo: ../../theme/codercub.jpg
    highlight-style: dracula
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-gradient: "linear-gradient(135deg, #0a0e1a 0%, #0d2137 60%, #1a1f35 100%)"

---

## Recap

- What did we learn?

## Exploring Patterns in Data

- Bring in diagrams from https://microbit.org/teach/lessons/exploring-patterns-in-data/

# What is a micro:bit?

## What is a micro:bit?

![](images/microbit-front.png){fig-align="center"}

## What is a micro:bit?

![](images/microbit-back.png){fig-align="center"}

## What is an Accelerometer?

- A small chip that measures acceleration
  - How fast moves from one speed to another
- Measures acceleration in 3 directions: X, Y, and Z
  - Measures both positive and negative direction

## What is an Accelerometer?

![](images/accelerometer.jpg){fig-align="center"}

# *If the micro:bit is plugged in, how do we move it around?*

## Introducing the micro:bit Wearable!

![](images/wearable.jpg){fig-align="center"}

## Introducing the micro:bit Wearable!

- TBD: diagram of wearable and receiver

# Our Plan

## Our Plan - pull in screenshots from CreateAI starter animation

1. Connect the micro:bits
2. Collect accelerometer data
3. Use this data to train a model
4. Test our model
5. (Tomorrow) Use the model in code

## Hands-on

- Get into pairs
- Pickup two micro:bits (one with and one without wearable)
- Wait for instructions! (We are going to do this together) 

## Steps - Connect the micro:bits

- Go to [createai.microbit.org](https://createai.microbit.org) - has to be using Chrome browser
- Create a project: "My First Project"
- Click on "Connect using micro:bit radio instead"
- Connect the collector (wearable) micro:bit and click "Next"
- Select the micro:bit connection from the the popup
- Disconnect and **very gently** insert the battery wire
- (Pause to make sure everyone sees smiley face)
- Next, connect the receiver micro:bit and click "Next"
- Select the connection again from the popup
- Put the wearable on one of your wrists (don't worry who is first, you'll both have a turn to collect data)
- Check that the graphs are moving correctly! Yes, you are connected and ready to record!

## Steps - Recording Data

- Create an action: "Waving"
- Record and repeat three times
- Pass the wearable to your partner. Have them record a different action. You can pick which action. Make sure your band is tight and don't hit anyone!
- (Pause to make sure everyone has two actions)
- Let's do another two. (One per person)
- (Pause to make sure everyone has four actions)

## Steps - Train the Model

- When ready, click on the "Train model" button

## Steps - Test the Model

- Take turns and test your actions. How well did the model learn? What happened if you just let your hand do nothing?

## Discussion - What did you find?

- What worked well?
- What didn't work well? Or what did it get wrong?
- How did it do on the actions that your partner recorded?

## Steps - Add Some More Data

- Click "Edit Data Samples" button
- Add an action for being still (if you don't already have one)
- Either re-record or add a new action. Try to have four or five.

## Diversity in Data

- Why is diversity important?
- TBD a slide or two on this, maybe some examples from AI failures - three good points here: https://microbit.org/teach/lessons/strengthening-models-through-adding-diverse-data/

## Steps - Swap with another group

- Take off the micro:bits and leave both of them on the table
- Make sure you are in the "Testing Model" screen
- Swap desks with another pair close to you
- Take turns to put on their micro:bit and test their actions

## Diversity Discussion

- What did you find?
- Did they work as you expected?

# Summary

- Summary of what we've covered today
- What we will do tomorrow

## Steps - Disconnecting the micro:bit

- Disconnect your receiver micro:bit and leave on the desk
- Leave your wearable micro:bit connected to the battery pack. I will disconnect this for you. (These can be easy to break)
